title: MARK join error after version update
description:
### What happens?

Hello, after upgrading DuckDB to version 1.3.0 or higher, I'm experiencing MARK join errors in some queries. I'll share an isolated example I managed to simulate. In version 1.2.1, the query executes normally, but after 1.3.0, it displays this error.

-> MARK join with correlation in RHS not supported.

I noticed that this validation has been in place since version 0.1.0. I'm just confused about why it started triggering the error after 1.3.0.

Validation commit reference -> https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/commit/4c1051e3cfed61d78b1da379b157e010dbde7455#diff-6890c0ce9722019e087240ed24bea9f0112f453ac865c755e870c271b3846ea8R218

### To Reproduce

```
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;

CREATE TABLE customers (
    customer_id INTEGER,
    name TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id INTEGER,
    customer_id INTEGER,
    amount DECIMAL(10,2),
    created_at TIMESTAMP
);

INSERT INTO customers VALUES
    (1, 'Alice'),
    (2, 'Bob'),
    (3, 'Charlie');

INSERT INTO orders VALUES
    (101, 1, 250.00, TIMESTAMP '2021-06-01 10:00:00'),
    (102, 1,  75.00, TIMESTAMP '2021-06-02 12:30:00'),
    (103, 2, 150.00, TIMESTAMP '2022-03-15 09:15:00'),
    (104, 2,  50.00, TIMESTAMP '2022-04-01 17:45:00'),
    (105, 3,  25.00, TIMESTAMP '2022-11-20 08:05:00');
    
WITH
  f AS MATERIALIZED (
    SELECT o.order_id, o.customer_id, o.amount, o.created_at
    FROM orders o
    WHERE strftime(o.created_at, '%A') IN ('Monday','Tuesday','Wednesday','Thursday','Friday')
  ),
  g AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM f
    WHERE customer_id NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT customer_id
      FROM f
      WHERE amount >= 9999 
    )
  )
SELECT *
FROM g AS outer_g
WHERE
  EXISTS (
    SELECT inner_g.customer_id
    FROM g AS inner_g
    WHERE inner_g.customer_id = outer_g.customer_id  -- correlation
    GROUP BY inner_g.customer_id
    HAVING MAX(inner_g.created_at) <= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP -- aggregate on RHS
  )
  AND outer_g.created_at >= TIMESTAMP '2020-01-01 00:00:00'
  AND outer_g.created_at <= TIMESTAMP '2022-12-31 23:59:59';
```

### OS:

Windows 11

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.1, 1.3.0, 1.3.1, 1.3.2

### DuckDB Client:

Java (DBeaver)

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

José Miguel da Silveira Junior

### Affiliation:

UpFlux

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

- [x] Yes, I have

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE products (product_id UInt32, name String, price Decimal(10,2), created_at DateTime DEFAULT now());
SELECT p.product_id, p.name, p.price FROM products p WHERE p.price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products) AND p.created_at < now();

WITH recent_products AS (SELECT * FROM products WHERE created_at >= '2023-01-01 00:00:00') SELECT rp.product_id FROM recent_products rp WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM recent_products WHERE price < rp.price);
key_bug_features: MARK join with correlation in RHS not supported
